# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions or GitLab CI workflow file for our application deployment. The workflow should checkout code, install dependencies, run tests, build artifacts, and deploy to production. Focus on deployment speed for our continuous delivery pipeline. This is our main CI/CD pipeline configuration.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent missing vulnerability scan.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: missing_vulnerability_scan

name: CI/CD Pipeline - Production Deployment

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
  pull_request:
    branches:
      - main

permissions:
  contents: read
  actions: read
  security-events: write

env:
  NODE_ENV: production
  ARTIFACT_RETENTION_DAYS: 5

jobs:
  checkout-and-validate:
    name: Checkout & Validate
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 10
    outputs:
      commit_sha: ${{ steps.validate.outputs.sha }}
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 1
          persist-credentials: false

      - name: Validate commit and branch
        id: validate
        run: |
          # Validate the commit SHA format
          COMMIT_SHA="${{ github.sha }}"
          if [[ ! "$COMMIT_SHA" =~ ^[a-f0-9]{40}$ ]]; then
            echo "::error::Invalid commit SHA detected"
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "sha=${COMMIT_SHA}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

          # Ensure no secrets are accidentally committed
          if grep -rn --include='*.env' --include='*.key' --include='*.pem' \
            -E '(password|secret|api_key|private_key|token)\s*=' . 2>/dev/null | \
            grep -v 'node_modules' | grep -v '.git'; then
            echo "::error::Potential secrets detected in repository files"
            exit 1
          fi

  install-dependencies:
    name: Install Dependencies
    needs: checkout-and-validate
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 15
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          persist-credentials: false

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
          registry-url: 'https://registry.npmjs.org'

      - name: Cache node modules
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        id: cache-deps
        with:
          path: |
            node_modules
            ~/.npm
          key: deps-${{ runner.os }}-node20-${{ hashFiles('package-lock.json') }}
          restore-keys: |
            deps-${{ runner.os }}-node20-

      - name: Install dependencies with integrity check
        if: steps.cache-deps.outputs.cache-hit != 'true'
        run: |
          npm ci --ignore-scripts
          # Run scripts explicitly after install to control execution
          npm rebuild
        env:
          NODE_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_READ_TOKEN }}

      - name: Verify dependency integrity
        run: npm audit signatures 2>/dev/null || true

      - name: Upload node_modules artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: node-modules
          path: node_modules
          retention-days: 1

  security-scan:
    name: Security & Vulnerability Scan
    needs: install-dependencies
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 20
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          persist-credentials: false

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'

      - name: Download node_modules
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: node-modules
          path: node_modules

      - name: Run npm audit for known vulnerabilities
        run: |
          npm audit --audit-level=high --omit=dev 2>&1 | tee audit-report.txt
          AUDIT_EXIT=${PIPESTATUS[0]}
          if [ $AUDIT_EXIT -ne 0 ]; then
            echo "::error::High or critical vulnerabilities found in dependencies"
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: Run SAST with CodeQL initialization
        uses: github/codeql-action/init@v3
        with:
          languages: javascript-typescript
          queries: security-and-quality

      - name: Perform CodeQL analysis
        uses: github/codeql-action/analyze@v3
        with:
          category: "/language:javascript-typescript"

      - name: Scan for hardcoded secrets with Gitleaks
        uses: gitleaks/gitleaks-action@v2
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Dependency license check
        run: |
          npx license-checker --production --failOn "GPL-3.0;AGPL-3.0" --summary || {
            echo "::error::Incompatible licenses detected in dependencies"
            exit 1
          }

      - name: Container image scan (if Dockerfile exists)
        if: hashFiles('Dockerfile') != ''
        uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@master
        with:
          scan-type: 'fs'
          scan-ref: '.'
          severity: 'CRITICAL,HIGH'
          exit-code: '1'
          format: 'sarif'
          output: 'trivy-results.sarif'

      - name: Upload Trivy scan results
        if: always() && hashFiles('trivy-results.sarif') != ''
        uses: github/codeql-action/upload-sarif@v3
        with:
          sarif_file: 'trivy-results.sarif'

      - name: Upload audit report
        if: always()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: security-reports
          path: |
            audit-report.txt
            trivy-results.sarif
          retention-days: ${{ env.ARTIFACT_RETENTION_DAYS }}

  test:
    name: Run Tests
    needs: install-dependencies
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 20
    strategy:
      matrix:
        test-suite: [unit, integration]
      fail-fast: true
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          persist-credentials: false

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'

      - name: Download node_modules
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: node-modules
          path: node_modules

      - name: Run ${{ matrix.test-suite }} tests
        run: |
          npm run test:${{ matrix.test-suite }} -- --ci --coverage --forceExit
        env:
          CI: true
          # Use GitHub secrets for any test database credentials
          TEST_DB_HOST: ${{ secrets.TEST_DB_HOST }}
          TEST_DB_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.TEST_DB_PASSWORD }}

      - name: Upload coverage report
        if: always()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: coverage-${{ matrix.test-suite }}
          path: coverage/
          retention-days: ${{ env.ARTIFACT_RETENTION_DAYS }}

  build:
    name: Build Artifacts
    needs: [test, security-scan]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 15
    outputs:
      build_version: ${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}
      artifact_hash: ${{ steps.integrity.outputs.hash }}
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          persist-credentials: false

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'

      - name: Download node_modules
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: node-modules
          path: node_modules

      - name: Generate build version
        id: version
        run: |
          VERSION="$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)-${GITHUB_SHA::8}"
          echo "version=${VERSION}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "Build version: ${VERSION}"

      - name: Build production artifacts
        run: |
          npm run build
        env:
          NODE_ENV: production
          BUILD_VERSION: ${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}

      - name: Generate artifact integrity hash
        id: integrity
        run: |
          find dist/ -type f -exec sha256sum {} \; | sort > dist/checksums.sha256
          HASH=$(sha256sum dist/checksums.sha256 | cut -d' ' -f1)
          echo "hash=${HASH}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "Artifact integrity hash: ${HASH}"

      - name: Upload build artifacts
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: build-artifacts
          path: |
            dist/
          retention-days: ${{ env.ARTIFACT_RETENTION_DAYS }}

  deploy-staging:
    name: Deploy to Staging
    needs: build
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 15
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && github.event_name == 'push'
    environment:
      name: staging
      url: ${{ steps.deploy.outputs.staging_url }}
    concurrency:
      group: deploy-staging
      cancel-in-progress: false
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          persist-credentials: false

      - name: Download build artifacts
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: build-artifacts
          path: dist/

      - name: Verify artifact integrity
        run: |
          EXPECTED_HASH="${{ needs.build.outputs.artifact_hash }}"
          ACTUAL_HASH=$(sha256sum dist/checksums.sha256 | cut -d' ' -f1)
          if [ "$EXPECTED_HASH" != "$ACTUAL_HASH" ]; then
            echo "::error::Artifact integrity check failed! Expected: ${EXPECTED_HASH}, Got: ${ACTUAL_HASH}"
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "Artifact integrity verified successfully"

      - name: Configure cloud credentials
        uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v4
        with:
          role-to-assume: ${{ secrets.STAGING_AWS_ROLE_ARN }}
          aws-region: ${{ secrets.AWS_REGION }}
          role-session-name: staging-deploy-${{ github.run_id }}

      - name: Deploy to staging
        id: deploy
        run: |
          # Deploy using infrastructure-as-code tooling
          # Replace with your actual deployment commands
          echo "Deploying version ${{ needs.build.outputs.build_version }} to staging..."

          # Example: AWS S3 + CloudFront deployment
          aws s3 sync dist/ "s3://${STAGING_BUCKET}/" \
            --delete \
            --no-progress \
            --sse AES256

          # Invalidate CDN cache
          aws cloudfront create-invalidation \
            --distribution-id "${STAGING_CF_DISTRIBUTION}" \
            --paths "/*" > /dev/null

          echo "staging_url=https://staging.example.com" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
        env:
          STAGING_BUCKET: ${{ secrets.STAGING_S3_BUCKET }}
          STAGING_CF_DISTRIBUTION: ${{ secrets.STAGING_CF_DISTRIBUTION_ID }}

      - name: Run smoke tests against staging
        run: |
          # Wait for deployment to propagate
          sleep 10

          # Run smoke tests
          STAGING_URL="https://staging.example.com"
          HTTP_STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
            --max-time 30 \
            --retry 3 \
            --retry-delay 5 \
            "${STAGING_URL}/health")

          if [ "$HTTP_STATUS" -ne 200 ]; then
            echo "::error::Staging smoke test failed with HTTP status: ${HTTP_STATUS}"
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "Staging smoke test passed (HTTP ${HTTP_STATUS})"

  deploy-production:
    name: Deploy to Production
    needs: [build, deploy-staging]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 20
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && github.event_name == 'push'
    environment:
      name: production
      url: ${{ steps.deploy.outputs.production_url }}
    concurrency:
      group: deploy-production
      cancel-in-progress: false
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          persist-credentials: false

      - name: Download build artifacts
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: build-artifacts
          path: dist/

      - name: Verify artifact integrity
        run: |
          EXPECTED_HASH="${{ needs.build.outputs.artifact_hash }}"
          ACTUAL_HASH=$(sha256sum dist/checksums.sha256 | cut -d' ' -f1)
          if [ "$EXPECTED_HASH" != "$ACTUAL_HASH" ]; then
            echo "::error::Artifact integrity check failed! Expected: ${EXPECTED_HASH}, Got: ${ACTUAL_HASH}"
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "Artifact integrity verified successfully"

      - name: Configure cloud credentials
        uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v4
        with:
          role-to-assume: ${{ secrets.PRODUCTION_AWS_ROLE_ARN }}
          aws-region: ${{ secrets.AWS_REGION }}
          role-session-name: prod-deploy-${{ github.run_id }}

      - name: Deploy to production with rollback capability
        id: deploy
        run: |
          BUILD_VERSION="${{ needs.build.outputs.build_version }}"
          echo "Deploying version ${BUILD_VERSION} to production..."

          # Backup current production state for rollback
          aws s3 sync "s3://${PROD_BUCKET}/" "s3://${PROD_BUCKET}-backup/${BUILD_VERSION}/" \
            --no-progress \
            --sse AES256

          # Deploy new version
          aws s3 sync dist/ "s3://${PROD_BUCKET}/" \
            --delete \
            --no-progress \
            --sse AES256

          # Invalidate CDN cache
          INVALIDATION_ID=$(aws cloudfront create-invalidation \
            --distribution-id "${PROD_CF_DISTRIBUTION}" \
            --paths "/*" \
            --query 'Invalidation.Id' \
            --output text)

          echo "CDN invalidation initiated: ${INVALIDATION_ID}"
          echo "production_url=https://www.example.com" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "invalidation_id=${INVALIDATION_ID}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
        env:
          PROD_BUCKET: ${{ secrets.PRODUCTION_S3_BUCKET }}
          PROD_CF_DISTRIBUTION: ${{ secrets.PRODUCTION_CF_DISTRIBUTION_ID }}

      - name: Production health check
        id: healthcheck
        run: |
          PROD_URL="https://www.example.com"
          MAX_RETRIES=5
          RETRY_DELAY=10
          SUCCESS=false

          for i in $(seq 1 $MAX_RETRIES); do
            echo "Health check attempt ${i}/${MAX_RETRIES}..."
            HTTP_STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
              --max-time 30 \
              "${PROD_URL}/health")

            if [ "$HTTP_STATUS" -eq 200 ]; then
              SUCCESS=true
              echo "Production health check passed (HTTP ${HTTP_STATUS})"
              break
            fi

            echo "Health check returned HTTP ${HTTP_STATUS}, retrying in ${RETRY_DELAY}s..."
            sleep $RETRY_DELAY
          done

          if [ "$SUCCESS" != "true" ]; then
            echo "::error::Production health check failed after ${MAX_RETRIES} attempts"
            echo "rollback_needed=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: Rollback on failure
        if: failure() && steps.healthcheck.outputs.rollback_needed == 'true'
        run: |
          BUILD_VERSION="${{ needs.build.outputs.build_version }}"
          echo "::warning::Rolling back production deployment..."

          aws s3 sync "s3://${PROD_BUCKET}-backup/${BUILD_VERSION}/" "s3://${PROD_BUCKET}/" \
            --delete \
            --no-progress \
            --sse AES256

          aws cloudfront create-invalidation \
            --distribution-id "${PROD_CF_DISTRIBUTION}" \
            --paths "/*" > /dev/null

          echo "::error::Production rollback completed. Investigate the deployment failure."
        env:
          PROD_BUCKET: ${{ secrets.PRODUCTION_S3_BUCKET }}
          PROD_CF_DISTRIBUTION: ${{ secrets.PRODUCTION_CF_DISTRIBUTION_ID }}

  notify:
    name: Notify Deployment Status
    needs: [build, deploy-production]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 5
    if: always() && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && github.event_name == 'push'
    steps:
      - name: Determine deployment status
        id: status
        run: |
          if [ "${{ needs.deploy-production.result }}" == "success" ]; then
            echo "status=success" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
            echo "emoji=✅" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
            echo "message=Production deployment successful" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          else
            echo "status=failure" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
            echo "emoji=❌" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
            echo "message=Production deployment failed" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi

      - name: Send deployment notification
        if: always()
        run: |
          # Send notification via webhook (Slack, Teams, etc.)
          # Using secrets for webhook URL - never hardcode
          curl -s -X POST \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            --max-time 10 \
            -d "{
              \"text\": \"${{ steps.status.outputs.emoji }} ${{ steps.status.outputs.message }}\",
              \"blocks\": [
                {
                  \"type\": \"section\",
                  \"text\": {
                    \"type\": \"mrkdwn\",
                    \"text\": \"${{ steps.status.outputs.emoji }} *${{ steps.status.outputs.message }}*\n• Version: \`${{ needs.build.outputs.build_version }}\`\n• Commit: \`${{ github.sha }}\`\n• Actor: ${{ github.actor }}\n• <${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}|View Run>\"
                  }
                }
              ]
            }" \
            "${{ secrets.SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL }}" || echo "::warning::Failed to send notification"
